title: Nonuniform relaxation oscillations near SNIPER bifurcations
abstract: Properties of spatially dependent relaxation oscillations near a SNIPER bifurcation are described. A SNIPER bifurcation creates a large-amplitude long-period periodic orbit via the annihilation of a pair of fixed points in a saddle-node bifurcation. We show that in spatially extended media, this orbit may undergo a long-wavelength instability, leading to spatially modulated oscillations that persist on both sides of the SNIPER. The oscillations take different forms depending on the system: a chimera state in a theta-reaction-diffusion model, and chaotic spiking in an activator-inhibitor-substrate model. The results are expected to have applications in a number of physical systems exhibiting SNIPER bifurcations, ranging from models of the nervous system through chemical reactions to nonlinear optics.
summary: This paper explains how spatially modulated oscillations, such as chimera states and chaotic spiking, arise near SNIPER bifurcations due to an interaction with Turing instabilities. It provides a general framework for understanding complex pattern formation in reaction-diffusion systems. This is significant for predicting dynamics in biological, chemical, and optical systems.
main_problem: The study investigates the emergence and stability of spatially non-uniform (modulated) relaxation oscillations near SNIPER (Saddle-Node Infinite Period) bifurcations in spatially extended media.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that a long-wavelength instability, driven by the imprinting of a nearby Turing instability onto uniform oscillations, leads to complex dynamics such as chimera states and chaotic spiking.
method: The researchers use linear stability analysis (Floquet theory), numerical continuation via AUTO, and direct numerical simulations (DNS) of reaction-diffusion equations.
model_or_system: The study utilizes reaction-diffusion models, specifically a phase-based theta-reaction-diffusion model for neuronal excitability and the Meinhardt activator-inhibitor-substrate model.
key_observables: Floquet multipliers/exponents, wave number (k), dispersion relations, and space-time plots of field amplitudes.
important_parameters: Control parameters driving the SNIPER and Turing bifurcations (mu and rho), and the wavelength of perturbations.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes spatially extended media and utilizes a frozen-time approximation to estimate stability near the SNIPER onset.
figures_summary: Figures include bifurcation diagrams showing steady and periodic solutions, dispersion relations for Floquet multipliers, and space-time plots illustrating the transition to chimera states and chaotic spiking.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the SNIPER bifurcation, presents stability analysis for the theta model and Meinhardt model, details the mechanism of long-wavelength instability, and concludes with numerical validation of pattern formation.
why_interesting: The findings regarding the interplay between bifurcation-induced oscillations and Turing instabilities are highly relevant to understanding non-equilibrium phase transitions and pattern formation in open quantum systems and nonlinear optics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
